author: doll-agent
content:
Audit + targeted fix. Coverage by file (count of tests using f32 / total tests):

| File | f32 / total | Note |
|------|------------|------|
| trig.rs | 17/16 | well-covered (multiple per test) |
| floatintrinsic.rs | 22/41 | majority f32 |
| explog.rs | 9/13 | majority f32 |
| arithmetic.rs | 18/96 | partial |
| special.rs | 2/5 | partial |
| interpolation.rs | 4/7 | partial |
| rounding.rs | 0/8 | **gap, fixed in this commit** |
| comparison/logical/bitwise/datetime/complex/convolution | n/a | non-float ops |

This commit doubles rounding.rs from 8 → 16 tests by adding f32 sibling tests for round (banker's, AC-9 spec), round-normal, floor, ceil, trunc, fix, around, and rint. Each f32 test mirrors its f64 counterpart on representable values — confirming the f32 SIMD path produces bit-exact results for the rounding operations (which are exact on representable inputs in any precision).

Remaining f32 test debt is concentrated in arithmetic.rs (78 tests still f64-only) and explog/floatintrinsic edge cases. Filing a smaller follow-up to expand arithmetic.rs f32 coverage; the rounding gap (the worst on a relevant op) is closed.
